verdicts.rationale: NPPES lists Medical Oncology, and the physician has an extensive documented history of breast cancer trial leadership (PALLAS, PACE, TRADE, TBCRC030/035) and breast cancer-focused publications, indicating a breast-focused oncology practice rather than incidental cases.
verdicts.requirement: The investigator must be a medical oncologist (or hematology-oncology specialist) with a breast cancer-focused practice, not a general oncologist with incidental breast cases.
verdicts.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists Hematology/Oncology or Medical Oncology, AND physician has a documented history of treating breast cancer patients (e.g., prior breast cancer trial roles or publications).

verdicts.rationale: Despite 117 publications and 11 confirmed trial roles reviewed, none address PIK3CA mutation, PI3K pathway inhibitors, or PI3K/AKT/mTOR-inhibitor trials; the topical focus is CDK4/6 inhibitors, ctDNA, immunotherapy, and PROs, not the PI3K pathway.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should have familiarity with PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer biology and biomarker-driven treatment selection, given this is a molecularly-selected trial.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician has authored/co-authored at least one publication on PIK3CA mutation, PI3K pathway inhibitors, or biomarker-selected HR+/HER2- breast cancer therapy, OR has prior investigator role on a PI3K/AKT/mTOR-inhibitor breast cancer trial.

verdicts.rationale: No self-reported caseload exists, but Medicare claims show a floor of 206 E&M services across 134 beneficiaries in a Medical Oncology practice, consistent with but not specific proof of a dedicated first-line metastatic HR+ breast cancer caseload.
verdicts.requirement: The practice must regularly see treatment-naive (first-line metastatic) HR+/HER2- breast cancer patients progressing on/after adjuvant endocrine therapy, since prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease is exclusionary.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician/practice can attest to managing a first-line metastatic HR+/HER2- breast cancer caseload sufficient to identify multiple eligible patients per year (self-reported caseload estimate), or Medicare claims show a floor volume of breast cancer E&M/chemotherapy administration codes consistent with a dedicated breast oncology practice.

verdicts.rationale: No self-reported attestation regarding tumor genomic testing workflow or PIK3CA testing infrastructure is present in the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: The site must have practical access to PIK3CA mutation testing (local CLIA-certified lab or ability to route samples to central/liquid biopsy testing) as part of routine or trial-driven workup.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site attests to an established workflow for tumor genomic testing (local NGS panel or central lab submission) as part of standard practice or via trial infrastructure.

verdicts.rationale: This criterion requires a self-reported attestation of oral oncolytic dispensing/IM fulvestrant administration capability, which is absent from the dossier despite indirect trial-role evidence of CDK4/6i experience.
verdicts.requirement: Site must have infrastructure for chronic oral oncolytic dispensing/adherence tracking (palbociclib, inavolisib/placebo) plus recurring intramuscular fulvestrant administration in 28-day cycles.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site attests to on-site or affiliated capability for IM drug administration and oral oncolytic dispensing/monitoring on a recurring cycle basis (e.g., existing CDK4/6 inhibitor + fulvestrant regimen experience).

verdicts.rationale: No self-report or attestation regarding hyperglycemia monitoring protocols or endocrinology referral pathways is present.
verdicts.requirement: Site should have capability to monitor and manage treatment-emergent hyperglycemia, including a defined post-treatment hyperglycemia follow-up pathway, given inavolisib's known metabolic toxicity.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site attests to protocols or endocrinology referral pathways for managing anti-hyperglycemic therapy and monitoring beyond standard oncology AE management.

verdicts.rationale: No explicit self-report attestation exists, but a co-authored publication on patient-reported outcomes in the SERENA-6 trial suggests some familiarity with administering PRO instruments in an industry trial context.
verdicts.requirement: Site must be able to administer and capture multiple patient-reported outcome instruments (QLQ-C30, QLQ-BR23, BPI-SF, PRO-CTCAE, EQ-5D-5L) per the study schedule.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site attests to existing PRO data collection infrastructure (electronic or paper) used in prior industry trials.

verdicts.rationale: No explicit attestation is present, but the physician's role in multi-year trials such as PALLAS (5-year follow-up published) and ongoing PACE/TRADE studies suggests established capacity for long-duration follow-up, short of a direct self-report.
verdicts.requirement: Site must sustain long-term survival follow-up (approx. every 3 months post-discontinuation, up to 3.7 years total study duration) via phone or clinic visits.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site attests to established procedures for long-duration survival follow-up contact and retention in prior long-term oncology trials.

verdicts.rationale: Physician is listed as overall official on multiple industry-sponsored breast cancer trials in ClinicalTrials.gov, including PALLAS, PACE, and TRADE.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience as an investigator on industry-sponsored oncology trials, given this is a large, complex Roche Phase II/III registration-track study with 135 global sites.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as principal investigator or sub-investigator on >=1 completed or ongoing industry-sponsored breast cancer clinical trial in clinicaltrials.gov.

verdicts.rationale: Open Payments records show multiple 2025 research payments from F. Hoffmann-La Roche AG (Roche/Genentech), directly satisfying the sponsor-relationship requirement.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should have a demonstrable relationship with breast oncology drug sponsors (e.g., Roche/Genentech or related CDK4/6i or PI3K-pathway drug makers), suggesting familiarity with sponsor trial conduct expectations.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research payments from Roche/Genentech or Pfizer (palbociclib manufacturer) within the last 5 years.

verdicts.rationale: A co-authored trial was explicitly conducted at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, an academic NCI-designated cancer center with established GCP-trained research infrastructure, matching the site-type requirement.
verdicts.requirement: Site should be part of an established research network or academic cancer center with GCP-trained staff capable of running a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled multi-drug trial.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician's practice matches or is affiliated with one of the listed site institutions (e.g., Sarah Cannon/Tennessee Oncology, Texas Oncology, academic medical center) or physician self-reports GCP certification and dedicated research staff.

narrative: This physician is a well-established Boston-based breast medical oncologist (likely Dana-Farber-affiliated) with a long track record as overall official on multiple industry-sponsored breast cancer trials (PALLAS, PACE, TRADE) and a demonstrable financial relationship with Roche, the trial sponsor — strong signals for trial execution and site-network fit. The clear weak spot is biomarker-specific expertise: across 117 publications and 11 confirmed trial roles, none touch PIK3CA mutation biology or PI3K/AKT/mTOR-pathway inhibitors, so the core scientific rationale for this molecularly-selected trial is unsupported by the evidence, despite adjacent expertise in CDK4/6i and endocrine-therapy-based first-line HR+ disease. Nearly all operational-capacity criteria (PIK3CA testing workflow, oral oncolytic/IM administration, hyperglycemia monitoring, PRO capture, long-term follow-up) rely on self-report data that is simply absent from this dossier, so those remain largely unknown or only weakly inferable from trial history; obtaining direct site-level attestations would substantially sharpen this picture, particularly around PI3K-pathway familiarity and metabolic AE management infrastructure.
